title: AI Genealogy Research Journal (frontmatter, v2)
description: JSON Schema for the YAML frontmatter of a per-person research journal (research/journals/@ID@.md). The journal is the single source of truth for OBSERVED PLATFORM CLAIMS, the classified DISAGREEMENTS, and the research TRAIL/STATUS; data/tree.json remains the conclusion store. This schema validates the parsed frontmatter object, NOT the prose body. It is a strict SUPERSET: additionalProperties:true so each tree's pre-v2 extra fields (priority, gps_compliance, lineage_branch, research_status, ...) stay valid. See methodology/07-cross-platform-reconciliation.md.

schema_version.description: Journal frontmatter schema version. v2 introduces platform_identity + disagreements + status_summary.

PersonId.description: Tree person id. Primary @I<digits>@; synthetic @I_<NAME>@ / @I_FS_*@; kindred also @FS_*@.

Platform.description: A platform leg in the claim/reconciliation comparison.

PlatformIdentity.description: Per-platform identity mapping: which external profile THIS person is, and whether that mapping is verified. Replaces the scattered fs_pid/wt_id/ancestry_id (genealogy) and familysearch/wikitree/findagrave/ancestry (kindred) fields. A wrong mapping is an IDENTITY-class disagreement.

Status.description: Disagreement lifecycle (DAG): open -> researching -> lead_found -> contributed -> resolved | held | disproven.

Class.description: The 7 disagreement classes. VITAL=birth/death date or place. PARENTAGE=a parent edge (incl. null-vs-named brick-wall leads). IDENTITY=a platform-id maps to a different person. CONFLATION=two people fused into one node. OVERCLAIM=confidence exceeds what sources prove. SOURCE=wrong-person/defective source. COVERAGE=platform not searched / no negative search.

by.description: Producer or human that wrote this entry, e.g. recon-walk, migration, brickwall-discriminator, human.

Disagreement.description: One cross-platform disagreement on one (cls, field) for this person. Idempotency key is (cls, field) within a journal — journal_io upserts by that key.

field.description: The specific field/subtype in conflict: a vital (birth_place, birth_date, death_date, death_place), a parent slot (father, mother), an identity locus (fs_mapping, wt_mapping), or a conformance check id (CONF-1, COV-1, ...).

values.description: The multi-platform comparison. Each leg is the value that platform asserts, or null where that leg has no value / wasn't compared (most are tree+familysearch today; ancestry/wikitree fill in as coverage grows).

verdict.description: The producer's machine verdict for this comparison.

next_record.description: The named primary record that would resolve this disagreement, if known.

StatusSummary.description: DERIVED roll-up of the disagreements[] statuses, recomputed by journal_io on every write. high_open + worst_open_severity are the inputs to the confidence-cap (JOUR-1).
